name: Skimmed Milk
reason: Low-fat dairy retaining most protein and calcium of whole milk. Supports bone health with fewer calories.

name: Skimmed Milk Powder
reason: Dehydrated low-fat dairy with useful protein and calcium. Processing reduces some nutritional integrity compared to fresh skimmed milk.

name: skimmed milk powder
reason: Dehydrated low-fat dairy with useful protein and calcium. Processing reduces some nutritional integrity compared to fresh skimmed milk.

name: Whole Milk Powder
reason: Dehydrated whole dairy with saturated fat and protein. Processing reduces nutritional quality compared to fresh whole milk.

name: Full Cream Milk
reason: Whole dairy with full fat content, rich in protein, calcium, and fat-soluble vitamins A and D.

name: Full Cream Milk Powder
reason: Dehydrated full-fat dairy. Retains protein and minerals but processing reduces nutritional quality versus fresh milk.

name: whey liquid
reason: Liquid dairy byproduct containing whey protein and lactose. Nutritional value is present but it is a heavily processed derivative.

name: Whey Liquid

name: whey powder
reason: Dried dairy byproduct with concentrated whey protein. Processed derivative with moderate nutritional value and potential allergen concerns.

name: whey permeate (milk)
reason: Highly processed dairy byproduct, mainly lactose and minerals with low protein content. Limited nutritional benefit.

name: Fresh Cream

name: Sucrose
reason: Refined table sugar composed of glucose and fructose. Provides only empty calories; excessive intake linked to metabolic disease.

name: Glucose
reason: Simple sugar used by the body for energy. Rapidly raises blood glucose; excessive intake may contribute to insulin resistance.

name: glucose
reason: Simple sugar used by the body for energy. Rapidly raises blood glucose; excessive intake may contribute to insulin resistance.

name: Glucose syrup
reason: Refined liquid sweetener derived from starch with high sugar content. Provides empty calories and raises blood glucose rapidly.

name: glucose syrup
reason: Refined liquid sweetener derived from starch with high sugar content. Provides empty calories and raises blood glucose rapidly.

name: Glucose Syrup
reason: Refined liquid sweetener derived from starch with high sugar content. Provides empty calories and raises blood glucose rapidly.

name: Glucose-Fructose Syrup
reason: Highly refined sweetener similar to high-fructose corn syrup. Frequent intake associated with metabolic syndrome and weight gain.

name: Liquid Glucose
reason: Refined liquid sweetener derived from starch with high sugar content. Provides empty calories and raises blood glucose rapidly.

name: Dextrose
reason: Pure glucose sugar derived from starch. Rapidly raises blood sugar levels; excessive intake may contribute to metabolic issues.

name: Dextrose monohydrate
reason: Pure glucose sugar derived from starch. Rapidly raises blood sugar levels; excessive intake may contribute to metabolic issues.

name: Lactose
reason: Natural milk sugar providing energy. May cause digestive discomfort in lactose-intolerant individuals; otherwise generally safe.

name: Steviol glycoside
reason: Plant-derived zero-calorie sweetener from stevia. Well-studied, generally recognized as safe, and does not raise blood glucose.

name: Steviol Glycoside
reason: Plant-derived zero-calorie sweetener from stevia. Well-studied, generally recognized as safe, and does not raise blood glucose.

reason: Plant-derived zero-calorie sweetener from stevia. Well-studied, generally recognized as safe, and does not raise blood glucose.

name: Sweetener (INS 960)
reason: Plant-derived zero-calorie sweetener from stevia. Well-studied, generally recognized as safe, and does not raise blood glucose.

name: Sweeteners (INS 950, INS 955)
reason: Combination of acesulfame K and sucralose, both approved synthetic sweeteners. Long-term high intake may affect gut microbiome.

name: Sweeteners (INS 955, 950)
reason: Combination of acesulfame K and sucralose, both approved synthetic sweeteners. Long-term high intake may affect gut microbiome.

name: Aspartame-INS951
reason: Synthetic sweetener approved by major food authorities. Possibly carcinogenic at very high intake per IARC 2023; safe at typical levels.

name: Acesulfame K - INS950
reason: Approved synthetic sweetener providing zero calories. Some animal studies suggest metabolic effects; considered safe at normal intake.

name: Acesulfame K
reason: Approved synthetic sweetener providing zero calories. Some animal studies suggest metabolic effects; considered safe at normal intake.

name: Sucralose
reason: Chlorinated synthetic sweetener approved globally. May alter gut microbiome with high long-term use; safe at typical intake levels.

name: Sucralose (INS 955)
reason: Chlorinated synthetic sweetener approved globally. May alter gut microbiome with high long-term use; safe at typical intake levels.
